subject: Re: Don't extract particles if close to the edge of the micrograph
body:
Hi Digvijay,
I think there are explicit checks in the program to exclude edge particles.
Your suggestion of padding out your tomos to include these particles seems
to be the way forward. But, i am not sure how far back you would beed to go
in the pipeline to inject this information into your data. Is there any
particular reason for retaining those edge pcles?

On Tue, 24 Aug 2021 at 23:40, Digvijay Singh <digvijay.in1@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Dimitry and the community,
>
> Re-hydrating this old thread.
>
> [1] Can I force WARP to even extract the particles that are close to the
> edge? Perhaps by padding the particleseries or subtilts of the particle
> with zeros or other suitable number?
>
> [2] When using CUBE, when particle cordinates are exported in .star
> format. The .star.bad file is created to exclude particles close to the
> edge or particles too close to each other, given the box size in CUBE ?
>
> [3] When re-constructing subtomograms using the .star file generated from
> other sources (like dynamo2m), I want to not exclude any particle, even the
> ones close to the edge of the tomogram? Is there a way to achieve this?
>
> Cheers,
> Digvijay
>
> On Friday, August 24, 2018 at 7:41:13 AM UTC-7 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> The follow-up messages were sent to my email address instead of this
>> thread. TL;DR: The particle diameter Matt selected for picking was smaller
>> than the actual particle, thus some positions were too close to the border.
>>
>>
>> On Wednesday, August 22, 2018 at 2:51:23 PM UTC+2, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>>
>>> Hey Matt,
>>>
>>> were those coordinates generated by Warp? Positions closer to the border
>>> than 80% of the particle diameter should be eliminated automatically. As
>>> long as you adhere to the "box = 3/2 diameter" rule of thumb, no boxes
>>> should touch the micrograph edge. If some do, it's a bug. However, having
>>> just checked the relevant code line, I can't see a way for it to fail.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>>
>>> On Wednesday, August 22, 2018 at 2:47:51 AM UTC+2,
>>> matthew....@monash.edu wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Hey mate,
>>>>
>>>> Sometimes we work with very large particles which also need a large
>>>> box. If a large portion of the box is outside the micrograph,
>>>> reconstructions don't always work great due to no Fourier information in
>>>> them as box padding isn't the greatest. Anyway, it would be cool to see an
>>>> option that automates this somewhat in WARP. Perhaps a button "don't
>>>> extract if on micrograph edge". I can always write a little script to
>>>> ignore the particles close the micrograph edge in in the star files, but it
>>>> a more user friendly option might be a good idea. Anyway, its just a
>>>> suggestion. The software is great man, we are using it to stream straight
>>>> off the microscope to assess how our data collections are going, its a real
>>>> game changer in my opinion. Good stuff!
>>>>
>>>> cheers
>>>>
>>>> matt B
>>>>
>>> --
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
> "Warp" group.
> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an
> email to warp-em+unsubscribe@googlegroups.com.

> .
>
-- 
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK
